c74object.name: fluid.dataset~
c74object.digest: A set of data associated with identifiers.
c74object.description: FluidDataSet is a container associating data points with identifiers.

discussion.p: Read more about fluid.dataset~ on the learn platform.
fluid.dataset~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: The name of the FluidDataSet. This is unique between all FluidDataSets.

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.dataset~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.dataset~ objects.

description.p: Add a new point to the FluidDataSet. The dimensionality of the FluidDataSet is governed by the size of the first point added. If the identifier already exists, or if the size of the data does not match the dimensionality of the FluidDataSet an error will be reported.

ul.li:
identifier:
The identifier for the point.

buffer:
A buffer~ containing the data for the point.

description.p: Retrieve a point from the data set into a buffer~. If the identifier does not exist an error will be reported.

ul.li:
identifier:
The identifier for the point to be retrieved.

buffer:
A buffer~ where the retrieved data will be stored.

description.p: Set the point. If the identifier exists, this method behaves like updatePoint. If the identifier doesn't exist, it behaves like addPoint.

ul.li:
identifier:
The identifier for this point.

buffer:
A buffer~ containing the data for the point.

description.p: Update an existing identifier's data. If the identifier does not exist, or if the size of the data does not match the dimensionality of the FluidDataSet an error will be reported.

ul.li:
identifier:
The identifier for this point.

buffer:
A buffer~ containing the data for the point.

description.p: Remove a point from the data set. If the identifier doesn't exist an error will be reported.

ul.li:
identifier:
The identifier to be deleted.

description.p: Merge sourceDataSet in the current DataSet. It will replace the value of points with the same identifier if overwrite is set to 1. ​To add columns instead, see the 'transformJoin' method of FluidDataSetQuery.

ul.li:
sourceDataSet:
The source DataSet to be merged.

overwrite:
A flag to allow overwrite points with the same identifier.

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

description.p: Post an abbreviated content of the DataSet in the window by default, but you can supply a custom action instead.
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset
The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: Import to the dataset the content of a buffer~, with optional transposition, and a map of frames/channels to the original IDs as a FluidLabelSet.

ul.li:
buffer:
The buffer to read from. The dataset will be resized.

transpose:
If 0, each buffer frame becomes a dataset point, and each buffer channel becomes a dataset dimension. If 1, channels become points, and frames become dimensions.

labelSet:
The FluidLabelSet from which to retrieve the point's IDs associated with their reference frame number (or channel number if transposed).

description.p: Dump the content of the dataset to a buffer~, with optional transposition, and a map of frames/channels to the original IDs as a FluidLabelSet.

ul.li:
buffer:
The buffer to write to. It will be resized.

transpose:
If 0, each dataset point becomes a buffer frame, and each dataset dimension becomes a buffer channel. If 1, points become channels, and dimensions become frames.

labelSet:
The FluidLabelSet in which to dump the point's IDs associated with their reference frame number (or channel number if transposed).

labelwise:
The reference order for the labelSet. By default (0), the frame number (or channel number if transposed) is the ID and the DataSet's IDs are the labels. When labelwise is set to 1, the original IDs are the IDs and the frame numbers (or channel numbers if transposed) are the labels.

description.p: Export the dataset identifier to a FluidLabelSet.

ul.li:
labelSet:
The FluidLabelSet to export to. Its content will be replaced.

description.p: Get the distances of the k nearest neighbours to a point. Note that this is a brute force distance measure, and comparatively inefficient for repeated queries against large datasets. For such cases, fluid.kdtree~ will be more efficient.

ul.li:
buffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point to match against. The number of frames in the buffer must match the dimensionality of the DataSet.

k:
The number of nearest neighbours to return. The identifiers will be sorted, beginning with the nearest.

description.p: Returns the identifiers of the k points nearest to the one passed. Note that this is a brute force distance measure, and comparatively inefficient for repeated queries against large datasets. For such cases, fluid.kdtree~ will be more efficient.

ul.li:
buffer:
A buffer~ containing a data point to match against. The number of frames in the buffer must match the dimensionality of the DataSet.

k:
The number of nearest neighbours to return. The identifiers will be sorted, beginning with the nearest.

description.p: Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
